title: doliocarpus dentatus subsp. ferrugineus
intro: Doliocarpus dentatus subsp. ferrugineus, also known as the ferrugineous doliocarpus, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly due to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits associated with the consumption of Doliocarpus dentatus subsp. ferrugineus include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water or by extracting its active compounds using a solvent, such as ethanol or methanol, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
